novel_features.name: Per-contact engagement scorecard
novel_features.description: Score every contact by opens, clicks, and inactive-since across all campaigns. The API has no engagement-history endpoint; we synthesize it locally.
novel_features.rationale: Requires joining synced campaign contact-reports with the contacts table — an answer no single EmailOctopus endpoint returns.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when an agent needs to find cold subscribers, build a reactivation cohort, or score engagement without paginating every campaign report by hand.
novel_features.group: Local joins the API can't do

novel_features.name: Cross-list duplicate finder
novel_features.description: Find contacts that appear on multiple lists in the account, optionally consolidating them onto one canonical list.
novel_features.rationale: EmailOctopus contacts are list-scoped; only a local cross-list join answers 'who's on more than one list'.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when an agent needs to clean up subscriber duplication, audit list sprawl, or plan a merge across multiple lists.
novel_features.group: Local joins the API can't do

novel_features.name: Campaign performance digest
novel_features.description: One-shot campaign report combining summary, top-N links, contact-level breakdown, and per-domain opens — rendered for terminal or Markdown paste.
novel_features.rationale: Joins three real report endpoints plus a local domain breakdown so a paste-ready report comes out of a single command instead of four browser tabs.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when an agent needs to summarize a campaign's results for a stakeholder doc without screen-scraping the EmailOctopus dashboard.

novel_features.name: List churn diff
novel_features.description: Show contacts touched in this list since a relative time. Surfaces the change-set the API can't return — useful for incremental syncs, audit logs, or alerting on recent activity.
novel_features.rationale: The EmailOctopus API has no updated_after filter or change feed; the only way to find recently-touched contacts is to compare local synced_at timestamps against a relative cutoff.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when an agent needs to see which contacts were touched in the last hour/day/week or run an incremental change-detection workflow.

novel_features.name: Tag set-algebra query
novel_features.description: Find contacts matching boolean combinations of tags: --has trial-started --not activated returns the trial cohort that hasn't converted.
novel_features.rationale: EmailOctopus has no segmentation API; set algebra over tags requires either client-side filtering of every contact or — like we do — local SQL.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when an agent needs to segment subscribers by tag combinations for targeted outreach, churn reactivation, or audience reporting.
novel_features.group: Local joins the API can't do

novel_features.name: CSV upsert with mapping
novel_features.description: Push a CSV into EmailOctopus with mapped fields and tags. Dry-runs the diff against the local store first, then chunks into batch-upsert calls paced under the rate limit.
novel_features.rationale: The native batch endpoint exists, but mapping a real CSV (Stripe export, Postgres dump, Notion table) into the right field/tag payload is hand-work today. Local-store dry-run shows the diff before any bytes hit the wire.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when an agent needs to atomically sync a CSV of contacts with tag/field mapping and pre-flight the change before applying it.

novel_features.name: Rate-budgeted bulk delete
novel_features.description: Delete many contacts matching a local predicate, paced under the 10/sec API limit with a resumable progress file.
novel_features.rationale: The EmailOctopus API has no bulk-delete endpoint; naive loops trip rate limits. Predicate resolution happens against the local store before any DELETE fires.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when an agent needs to clean up unsubscribed, bounced, or stale contacts in bulk without hitting 429s or losing progress mid-run.

novel_features.name: Bulk automation trigger
novel_features.description: Queue an automation for many contacts from stdin or CSV, paced under the rate limit with retry on 429.
novel_features.rationale: The native queue endpoint accepts one contact at a time; bulk triggers from app events (trial-end, plan upgrade, win-back) require client-side fan-out with backoff.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when an agent needs to start an automation for a batch of contacts (trial-ending cohort, plan-upgrade celebration) without writing loop+backoff boilerplate.

narrative.headline: Every EmailOctopus v2 endpoint, plus the cross-list joins, churn diffs, and rate-budgeted bulk operations the API can't do on its own.
narrative.value_prop: EmailOctopus is the only major email-marketing platform whose free tier includes API access — but until now no maintained v2 CLI or SDK existed. This CLI ships all 25 endpoints with --json, --select, --csv, --dry-run, plus a local SQLite store that powers cross-list dedupe, per-contact engagement scoring, list churn diffs over time, and rate-budgeted bulk delete.
narrative.auth_narrative: Generate a v2 key at https://api.emailoctopus.com/developer/api-keys/create and export it as `EMAILOCTOPUS_API_KEY`. Keys created before October 2024 are v1-only and will return 401 against the v2 API — regenerate if you see auth errors.

quickstart.comment: Verify auth and reachability before doing anything else.
Inventory your lists and grab the list_id you'll use below.
Pull every list, contact, tag, field, and campaign into the local SQLite store.
Surface cold subscribers — the API has no equivalent.
Paste-ready campaign report in one command.

troubleshoots.fix: Your API key was minted before October 2024. Regenerate it at https://api.emailoctopus.com/developer/api-keys/create and re-export EMAILOCTOPUS_API_KEY.
Use `contacts bulk-delete --rate 8` or `automations trigger-batch` — both pace under the documented 10 req/sec budget and respect the X-RateLimit-Retry-After header.
Run `emailoctopus-pp-cli sync --full` first. Engagement scoring joins local campaign-report tables that only populate after sync.
Always run with `--dry-run` first; the dry-run diff against the local store shows exactly which contacts will be created, updated, or no-op'd before any API call fires.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when an agent or operator needs the full EmailOctopus v2 surface — lists, contacts, tags, custom fields, campaign reports, and automation triggers — plus the local-join queries the hosted API can't answer (cross-list duplicates, per-contact engagement scoring, list churn since the last snapshot, tag set-algebra). Especially valuable for indie newsletter operators, small-SaaS growth engineers, and agencies running many client lists, since it works offline once synced and paces all mutations under the documented 10 req/sec rate limit.

recipes.title: Find cold subscribers across a list
recipes.explanation: Joins synced contacts with all campaign contact-reports to surface anyone who hasn't opened or clicked in 90 days. The --select narrows the response to just the fields an agent needs, dropping the ~10KB of nested per-campaign detail.

recipes.title: Generate a paste-ready campaign report
recipes.explanation: One command renders summary metrics, top-clicked links, per-domain opens, and unsubscribe breakdown as Markdown — paste straight into Notion or a stakeholder doc.

recipes.title: Find contacts on more than one list
recipes.explanation: Local SQL groups contacts by lowercased email across every synced list, returning the duplicates with their list memberships. Useful before consolidating lists or auditing subscriber sprawl.

recipes.title: Segment by tag set-algebra
recipes.explanation: Returns the contacts tagged trial-started but not activated — the cohort that's still in trial but hasn't converted. EmailOctopus has no native segmentation API.

recipes.title: Bulk delete with a safety net
recipes.explanation: Resolves the predicate against the local store and shows what would be deleted. Drop --dry-run to actually delete, paced at 8 req/sec under the 10/sec API limit, with a resumable progress file in case the run is interrupted.

recipes.title: Diff a list to see what changed
recipes.explanation: Compares the current state to yesterday's sync snapshot. Classifies contacts as added, removed, status-changed, or tag-changed — the change feed the API doesn't provide.
